new_classes.label_en: Waters incl. mineral waters and aerated waters, containing added sugar or other sweetening matter or flavoured, and other non-alcoholic beverages (excl. fruit/vegetable juices of 2009)
new_classes.label_de: Wasser, einschl. Mineralwasser und kohlensäurehaltiges Wasser, mit Zusatz von Zucker, anderen Süßmitteln oder Aromastoffen, und andere nicht alkoholhaltige Getränke (ausgen. Frucht- und Gemüsesäfte der Pos. 2009)
new_classes.definition_en: Waters (including mineral waters and aerated waters) containing added sugar, other sweetening matter or flavouring, and other non-alcoholic beverages as defined in Chapter 22 Note 3, excluding fruit or vegetable juices of heading 2009. These are liquids presented ready for drinking.
A quality borne by a beverage indicating that it is non-alcoholic as defined in Chapter 22, Note 3 of the CN (actual alcoholic strength by volume not exceeding 0.5% vol).
A quality borne by a water-based beverage indicating the presence of added sugar, other sweetening matter, or flavouring substances.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) the beverage is non-alcoholic (NonAlcoholicBeverageQuality), (2) it contains added sugar, sweetening matter or flavouring (FlavoredOrSweetenedWaterQuality), and (3) it is not a fruit/vegetable juice of heading 2009 (expressed as complement of 2201 as a proxy, though a more precise exclusion of heading 2009 cannot be fully formalised without a FruitVegetableJuice2009 class in the TBox). The exclusion of heading 2009 juices and the precise Chapter 22 Note 3 definition of 'non-alcoholic beverage' (≤ 0.5% vol) cannot be fully axiomatised without a dedicated alcoholic-strength range restriction for 0–0.5% vol and a FruitVegetableJuice2009 exclusion class. Coverage is reduced accordingly. Additionally, the legal text notes that these beverages must be liquids presented ready for drinking, which is a functional characteristic that is not directly modelled in the current TBox.
